summary:
The extensor hallucis longus does not contribute to foot inversion. Among these muscles, it uniquely focuses on extending the big toe rather than aiding in turning the sole inward or outward during movement. \boxed{\text{The extensor hallucis longus}} works differently from others listed here by exclusively targeting the big toe’s motion without involvement in foot inversion processes. 

Note how I condensed Agent2's detailed explanation into two concise yet informative statements focusing directly on what was asked about specific functions related to foot inversion among given options.
